headline: Digital Bank Licences See 780,000 Sign-Ups in Year One
context: Malaysia's five licensed digital banks collectively onboarded 780,000 customers in their first year of operations ending March 2026, with GXBank leading at 320,000 accounts but profitability still years away.

cards.big: 780,000 Malaysians joined digital banks — but 62% opened accounts only for sign-up bonuses
cards.sub: Average deposit balance of RM340 per account suggests curiosity, not genuine banking migration

cards.big: 5 digital banks collectively hold RM1.2 billion in deposits versus RM2.1 trillion in traditional bank system
cards.sub: Market share of 0.06% after 12 months raises questions about whether digital banks can achieve scale

cards.big: Digital banks target 4.9 million unbanked Malaysians — but 73% of sign-ups so far have existing bank accounts
cards.sub: Financial inclusion mandate not being met when existing banked customers dominate early adoption

cards.big: BNM granted 5-year regulatory sandbox allowing digital banks to operate with lower capital requirements
cards.sub: Consumer protection frameworks still reference traditional banking — no specific digital bank complaint mechanism exists

cards.big: Digital banks were sold as financial inclusion tools — so far they're just another app for the already-banked
Should digital banks face annual financial inclusion targets — 30% unbanked sign-ups — or risk licence review?
